title: Year 7 Health & Physical Education: Traditional Methods and Practices - Enhanced by General_Kaitiaki
subject: Health & Physical Education
culturalContext: Developing literacy skills through stories, both traditional pūrākau and contemporary narratives that connect to our local community and cultural heritage.
learningObjectives: Develop deep understanding of health & physical education concepts through cultural and local context
Apply traditional knowledge and modern methods to health & physical education challenges
Connect learning to local environment, community, and cultural heritage
Demonstrate respect for Māori and Pacific perspectives and values
Use Te Reo Māori appropriately in health & physical education contexts

activities.title: Whakawhanaungatanga: Building Connections
activities.description: Begin with karakia and introductions. Students share their prior knowledge, experiences, and personal connections to the topic. Establish cultural protocols and respectful learning environment.

activities.title: Cultural Context Exploration
activities.description: Students will create digital stories combining traditional Māori storytelling techniques with modern multimedia tools, focusing on local legends and community narratives.

activities.title: Hands-on Learning and Application
activities.description: Practical application of health & physical education concepts using both traditional and modern methods. Students work collaboratively to solve real-world problems connected to their local community.

activities.title: Reflection and Cultural Connection
activities.description: Students reflect on their learning journey, connecting new knowledge to cultural values, community needs, and personal growth. Share insights and celebrate achievements.

activities.title: Action and Community Impact
activities.description: Students identify ways to apply their learning to benefit their community, family, or local environment. Plan and implement small-scale actions that demonstrate kaitiakitanga.

tasks: Formative: Teacher observation during activities, discussions, and collaborative work
Formative: Peer feedback and self-reflection on learning progress
Summative: Student presentation demonstrating understanding, cultural connection, and community application
Summative: Portfolio of work showing growth in knowledge, skills, and cultural understanding

nzcAlignment: Te Tiriti o Waitangi: Cultural narratives and perspectives
